title: ELV-P4-CHUNK-001
description:
[NULL] stripped the exception handling from the ID scanner. It now crashes on any non-integer input and floods the audit log with errors. Restore the three blocks — every scan must be logged, pass or fail.

Fill in the three snippets inside parseStudentID(String input):
- snippet_1: Parse input as an integer. Return "VALID:" + the value if it is between 10000 and 99999 (inclusive), otherwise return "INVALID:OUT_OF_RANGE".
- snippet_2: Return "INVALID:NOT_A_NUMBER" when the input cannot be parsed as an integer.
- snippet_3: Always print "scan attempt logged" — this runs whether parsing succeeded or failed.

Input: a string representing a student ID
Output: "scan attempt logged" on its own line, then the validation result on the next line

java.name: Java Implementation

snippets.snippet_1: Parse the input String as an integer. If the value is in range [10000, 99999], return "VALID:" + that integer. Otherwise return "INVALID:OUT_OF_RANGE".
snippets.snippet_3: Print "scan attempt logged" to standard output.

title: ELV-P4-CHUNK-002
description:
The room code scanner is throwing unhandled exceptions on every bad input. [NULL] knew exactly which lines to delete to break the audit trail. Restore all three exception blocks.

Fill in the three snippets inside parseRoomCode(String input):
- snippet_1: Parse input as an integer. Return "ROOM:" + the value if it is between 100 and 999 (inclusive), otherwise return "INVALID:NOT_A_ROOM".
- snippet_2: Return "INVALID:FORMAT_ERROR" when the input cannot be parsed as an integer.
- snippet_3: Always print "door scan logged" — this runs whether parsing succeeded or failed.

Input: a string representing a room code
Output: "door scan logged" on its own line, then the validation result on the next line

java.name: Java Implementation

snippets.snippet_1: Parse the input String as an integer. If the value is in range [100, 999], return "ROOM:" + that integer. Otherwise return "INVALID:NOT_A_ROOM".
snippets.snippet_3: Print "door scan logged" to standard output.

title: ELV-P4-CHUNK-003
description:
The port scanner crashes on anything that is not a clean integer. Without proper exception handling the network audit trail is blind. Restore it.

Fill in the three snippets inside parsePort(String input):
- snippet_1: Parse input as an integer. Return "PORT:" + the value if it is between 1 and 65535 (inclusive), otherwise return "INVALID:PORT_OUT_OF_RANGE".
- snippet_2: Return "INVALID:NOT_A_PORT" when the input cannot be parsed as an integer.
- snippet_3: Always print "connection attempt logged" — this runs whether parsing succeeded or failed.

Input: a string representing a port number
Output: "connection attempt logged" on its own line, then the validation result on the next line

java.name: Java Implementation

snippets.snippet_1: Parse the input String as an integer. If the value is in range [1, 65535], return "PORT:" + that integer. Otherwise return "INVALID:PORT_OUT_OF_RANGE".
snippets.snippet_3: Print "connection attempt logged" to standard output.

title: ELV-P4-CHUNK-004
description:
The elevator rejects every floor request because the parser throws on bad input. [NULL] removed the safety net. Restore the exception blocks — the finally block is not optional, the lift log must always write.

Fill in the three snippets inside parseFloor(String input):
- snippet_1: Parse input as an integer. Return "FLOOR:" + the value if it is between 1 and 10 (inclusive), otherwise return "INVALID:NO_SUCH_FLOOR".
- snippet_2: Return "INVALID:NOT_A_NUMBER" when the input cannot be parsed as an integer.
- snippet_3: Always print "elevator request logged" — this runs whether parsing succeeded or failed.

Input: a string representing a floor number
Output: "elevator request logged" on its own line, then the validation result on the next line

java.name: Java Implementation

snippets.snippet_1: Parse the input String as an integer. If the value is in range [1, 10], return "FLOOR:" + that integer. Otherwise return "INVALID:NO_SUCH_FLOOR".
snippets.snippet_3: Print "elevator request logged" to standard output.

title: ELV-P5-CHUNK-001
description:
4,847 log entries and the threat filter is gone — [NULL] deleted it first. Without it the emergency system cannot assess severity. Find the threats — the building needs this report before it can act.

Fill in the three snippets inside analyzeLogs(ArrayList<String> logs):
- snippet_1: Declare an int counter starting at 0 and an ArrayList<String> to collect threat descriptions.
- snippet_2: If the current entry starts with "THREAT:", increment the counter and add the substring after the first 8 characters to the description list.
- snippet_3: Return "THREATS:0|none" if the counter is 0, otherwise return "THREATS:" + count + "|" + descriptions joined by ",".

java.name: Java Implementation

snippets.snippet_1: Declare: int count = 0; and ArrayList<String> threats = new ArrayList<>();
snippets.snippet_2: Check if entry starts with "THREAT:". If so, increment count and add entry.substring(8) to threats.
snippets.snippet_3: If count == 0 return "THREATS:0|none", else return "THREATS:" + count + "|" + String.join(",", threats).

title: ELV-P5-CHUNK-002
description:
The server error filter was wiped along with the intrusion logs. Critical errors are invisible to the monitoring system. Restore the filter — find every ERROR entry and report the count.

Fill in the three snippets inside filterErrors(ArrayList<String> logs):
- snippet_1: Declare an int counter starting at 0 and an ArrayList<String> to collect error messages.
- snippet_2: If the current entry starts with "ERROR:", increment the counter and add the substring after the first 7 characters to the message list.
- snippet_3: Return "ERRORS:0|none" if the counter is 0, otherwise return "ERRORS:" + count + "|" + messages joined by ",".

java.name: Java Implementation

snippets.snippet_1: Declare: int count = 0; and ArrayList<String> errors = new ArrayList<>();
snippets.snippet_2: Check if entry starts with "ERROR:". If so, increment count and add entry.substring(7) to errors.
snippets.snippet_3: If count == 0 return "ERRORS:0|none", else return "ERRORS:" + count + "|" + String.join(",", errors).

title: ELV-P5-CHUNK-003
description:
The badge access log is full of denied entries — someone was probing every door. The scanner that should have flagged them is offline. Find every DENIED entry before [NULL] makes another move.

Fill in the three snippets inside findDenied(ArrayList<String> entries):
- snippet_1: Declare an int counter starting at 0 and an ArrayList<String> to collect denied entries.
- snippet_2: If the current entry contains the word "DENIED" anywhere, increment the counter and add the full entry to the list.
- snippet_3: Return "DENIED:0|none" if the counter is 0, otherwise return "DENIED:" + count + "|" + entries joined by ",".

java.name: Java Implementation

snippets.snippet_1: Declare: int count = 0; and ArrayList<String> denied = new ArrayList<>();
snippets.snippet_2: Check if entry contains "DENIED". If so, increment count and add the full entry to denied.
snippets.snippet_3: If count == 0 return "DENIED:0|none", else return "DENIED:" + count + "|" + String.join(",", denied).

expectation.output:
DENIED:2|Student 002: DENIED low clearance,Visitor 01: DENIED no badge
DENIED:0|none

title: ELV-P5-CHUNK-004
description:
Elevator requests are piling up but the floor filter is down. Security needs to know exactly how many calls are going to each floor. Restore the filter — match by floor number and report the count.

Fill in the three snippets inside findFloorRequests(ArrayList<String> logs, int floor):
- snippet_1: Declare an int counter starting at 0 and an ArrayList<String> to collect matching requests.
- snippet_2: If the current entry ends with ":" + floor, increment the counter and add the full entry to the list.
- snippet_3: Return "FLOOR" + floor + ":0|none" if the counter is 0, otherwise return "FLOOR" + floor + ":" + count + "|" + entries joined by ",".

java.name: Java Implementation

snippets.snippet_1: Declare: int count = 0; and ArrayList<String> matches = new ArrayList<>();
snippets.snippet_2: Check if entry ends with ":" + floor. If so, increment count and add the full entry to matches.
snippets.snippet_3: If count == 0 return "FLOOR" + floor + ":0|none", else return "FLOOR" + floor + ":" + count + "|" + String.join(",", matches).

expectation.output:
FLOOR2:2|User A requested floor:2,User C requested floor:2
FLOOR5:0|none
